definition: to accept or admit that something exists or is true

sentences.text: The report _____ several weaknesses in the current system.
She refused to _____ that a mistake had been made.
The company _____ the contribution of its employees.

definition: to expect something and prepare for it in advance

sentences.text: We must _____ possible delays before starting the project.
The team _____ a rise in demand next year.
She had not _____ such a strong reaction.

definition: to make an idea or statement easier to understand

sentences.text: Could you _____ what you mean by that statement?
The manager _____ the new procedure during the meeting.
This diagram _____ how the system operates.

definition: to act according to a rule, request, or standard

sentences.text: All employees must _____ with the safety regulations.
The product fully _____ with international standards.
The company failed to _____ with the new law.

definition: to communicate or express an idea, feeling, or message

sentences.text: The graph clearly _____ the change in customer behaviour.
It is difficult to _____ complex ideas in a short email.
Her tone _____ a sense of urgency.

definition: to become smaller, weaker, or less; or to refuse politely

sentences.text: Sales began to _____ after the price increase.
He politely _____ the invitation.
The report shows that demand is _____ steadily.

definition: to obtain something from a particular source

sentences.text: Many English words _____ from Latin.
The company _____ most of its income from exports.
The conclusion was _____ from several studies.

definition: to become or make something smaller or less important

sentences.text: The effect of the medicine will gradually _____.
Poor communication _____ trust within a team.
Her enthusiasm had _____ by the end of the project.

definition: to give special importance or attention to something

sentences.text: The trainer _____ the importance of regular practice.
I want to _____ one key point before we continue.
The report _____ the need for better planning.

definition: to improve the quality, value, or effectiveness of something

sentences.text: The new design will _____ the user experience.
Training _____ employee performance.
The image was digitally _____ before publication.

definition: to make a process or action easier

sentences.text: The new platform will _____ communication between teams.
Clear instructions _____ faster learning.
The workshop was designed to _____ cooperation.

definition: to rise and fall irregularly in amount or level

sentences.text: Energy prices often _____ throughout the year.
The temperature _____ between ten and twenty degrees.
Demand is likely to _____ in the coming months.

definition: to put a plan, decision, or system into effect

sentences.text: The company plans to _____ a new training program.
The changes were _____ gradually.
Management _____ the policy across all departments.

definition: to suggest something without stating it directly

sentences.text: His comment seemed to _____ that the plan had failed.
The results _____ a connection between the two factors.
She _____ that more changes were necessary.

definition: to give a convincing reason for an action or decision

sentences.text: Can you _____ the additional cost?
The results do not _____ changing the entire system.
He _____ his decision with clear evidence.

definition: to discuss something in order to reach an agreement

sentences.text: The two companies are trying to _____ a new contract.
She _____ better working conditions for the team.
Both sides agreed to _____ further.

definition: to notice, understand, or interpret something in a particular way

sentences.text: Customers often _____ the brand as reliable.
He _____ the criticism as unfair.
The change may be _____ differently by each group.

definition: to decide which tasks or issues are most important

sentences.text: We need to _____ the most urgent problems first.
She _____ customer safety over speed.
The team _____ tasks according to risk.

definition: to keep or continue to have something

sentences.text: The company wants to _____ experienced employees.
This material _____ heat very well.
She _____ full control of the project.

definition: to examine and improve something by making changes

sentences.text: You should _____ your notes before the exam.
The team _____ the schedule after the delay.
The document is being _____ by the legal department.

definition: happening after something else

sentences.text: The first test failed, but _____ trials were successful.
The decision affected all _____ stages of the project.
She explained the issue in a _____ meeting.

definition: to maintain something over a period of time

sentences.text: The company must _____ growth without reducing quality.
Regular practice helps _____ motivation.
The system cannot _____ this level of demand.

definition: to experience a process, change, or difficult situation

sentences.text: The building will _____ major repairs next year.
The product _____ several safety tests.
The company is _____ a period of rapid change.

definition: to confirm that something is correct, effective, or acceptable

sentences.text: Further testing is needed to _____ the results.
The experiment _____ the original theory.
The software automatically _____ each entry.

definition: existing or happening in many places or among many people

sentences.text: The new technology gained _____ acceptance.
There is _____ concern about data security.
The problem is more _____ than expected.
